quote: Four verse lines displayed as one morphemic line. The tail verb inkar=an '(I) looked' at the end of line 1 is repeated as the head of line 3 (inkar=an kuni 'that (I) would look'), with the awa discovery connective bridging the juncture. somosuykusun 'something beyond imagining' is a lexical expression whose internal morphology is opaque in the accessible sources; the gloss follows Nakagawa's Japanese rendering まさかそのようなものを. rok is a perfect marker.

suggestion: Clarify how the four verse lines are represented in the provided example, or adjust the note to accurately reflect the example.
